title: What Breast Cancer Cost Us in India — and What It Did to My FIRE Plan
meta_title: Breast Cancer Treatment Cost in India Without Insurance (2026)
subtitle: The real, itemised out-of-pocket cost of a triple-negative breast cancer treatment in Pune — and what a ₹40-lakh bill does to a retirement plan.
description: The real, itemised cost of breast cancer treatment in India without insurance (Pune, 2026): chemotherapy, immunotherapy, surgery, radiation and scans — close to ₹40 lakh — plus how we cut the bill and what it did to a FIRE plan.

faqs.question: How much does breast cancer treatment cost in India without insurance?
faqs.answer: For a full triple-negative breast cancer protocol (chemotherapy, immunotherapy, surgery, radiation and a year of follow-up), the full sticker price at a private Pune hospital in 2026 is close to ₹40 lakh (about $42,000), with immunotherapy (pembrolizumab) alone around ₹34 lakh. But the amount you actually pay can be far lower — manufacturer access programs like Keytruda / Kiran can mean paying for only about 5 of 17 immunotherapy doses, cutting roughly ₹24 lakh off the bill. Costs vary widely by hospital, city, stage and regimen.

faqs.question: Why is immunotherapy (Keytruda / pembrolizumab) so expensive in India?
faqs.answer: Pembrolizumab runs roughly ₹2 lakh (~$2,100) per dose, and a standard breast-cancer protocol uses about 17 doses — around ₹34 lakh (~$36,000) gross, the single largest cost of treatment. But manufacturer access programs such as Keytruda / Kiran can mean paying for only about 5 of the 17 doses — a saving of roughly ₹24 lakh (~$25,000). Always ask about the access program before you pay.

faqs.question: How much does chemotherapy cost per cycle in India?
faqs.answer: Roughly ₹47,000 (~$500) per cycle at a private hospital in Pune in 2026, with a minimum of about 8 cycles (around ₹3.8 lakh, ~$4,000, for chemotherapy alone), plus about ₹3,000 (~$32) a week in blood tests and supportive medication during treatment.

faqs.question: How much does a PET-CT scan cost in India?
faqs.answer: About ₹16,000 (~$170) per scan. You may need it a couple of times in the first year, then roughly once a year afterwards to monitor for recurrence.

faqs.question: How can I reduce cancer treatment costs in India?
faqs.answer: The biggest lever is the immunotherapy access program (e.g. Keytruda / Kiran), which can mean paying for only about 5 of 17 pembrolizumab doses — a saving of roughly ₹24 lakh. Then buy generic medicines from an outside pharmacy rather than the hospital pharmacy, and ask about hospital charity and NGO schemes (often another ₹2–5 lakh). Apply before you start paying.

faqs.question: Can you buy health insurance after a cancer diagnosis?
faqs.answer: Effectively no. Once there is any finding in your medical file — even a benign one — insurers will decline or exclude it. We applied within days of a benign first biopsy and were still rejected. Insurance must be bought while you are healthy.
